payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Layer 1 Postgres ledger/job lane with DB-enforced idempotency, SKIP LOCKED multi-claimer claims, and run-partitioned catch-up while keeping SQLite default.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: All 5 PRD promise contracts have public boundary + allowed/forbidden outcomes and map to current source
SQLite remains default; Postgres opt-in via URL scheme
Out-of-Scope and Testing Decisions explicit

criteria: PRD promises map to real source boundaries: met
Acceptance criteria per promise present: met
evidence: tests/test_postgres_ledger_lane.py (SQL-shape always-on; live-PG gated on CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN)
confidence.rationale: Every PRD promise re-verified against current source via Read/Grep and git status matches; reduced from higher because PRD sha256 not re-confirmed (shasum needs approval), live-Postgres concurrency is self_reported, and pytest not re-run this gate per policy.

payload.content: PRD for the Layer 1 Postgres ledger/job lane is well-formed and all 5 promise contracts map to real, matching source boundaries (SQLite default routing, partial-unique idempotency, SKIP LOCKED materialized CTE with limit+uniform order, per-run previous_event_id chain, make migrate + PgBouncer docs). ACCEPT.

assumptions_to_verify: Inline DDL in postgres_state.py and migrations/ stay in lockstep (drift-watch at execution/outcome)
make migrate cleanly upgrades a real Postgres and leaves SQLite users unaffected
contradictions_checked: Memory pinned partial-unique index at :116-118 but current source has it at :147-149 — substance identical, only line numbers shifted (no contradiction)
claim CTE filters recovery_point IN ('reserved','request_written') consistent with non-terminal idempotency semantics
SQLite-default routing confirmed unchanged — no contradiction with P1 forbidden outcomes
missing_evidence: PRD sha256 not re-confirmed against handoff (shasum approval-gated)
Live multi-writer idempotency-race and SKIP LOCKED disjointness only assertable with a real Postgres DSN (gated, self_reported)
pytest not executed this gate
critical_review.strongest_objection: Dual schema source-of-truth: tables are defined both inline in postgres_state.py and in the Alembic migration; P5 forbids hidden schema drift, so divergence between the two would silently violate the PRD at runtime.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the inline DDL and Alembic migration defined materially different schemas, or if a promise contract lacked a corresponding source boundary, or if the on-disk PRD hash mismatched the handoff packet.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Layer 1 Postgres ledger/job lane with DB-enforced idempotency, SKIP LOCKED multi-claimer claims, and run-partitioned catch-up while keeping SQLite default.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: All 4 slices are well-formed with required fields
Coverage Index maps P1-P5 with no orphan promises
Every AC traces to a real source boundary in current artifacts
Multi-writer idempotency is DB-enforced, test is non-vacuous

criteria: All P1-P5 promises have a covering slice
Each AC maps to an existing source boundary
Multi-writer test uses real concurrency + DB enforcement
confidence.rationale: All slice ACs verified against current source line-by-line and Coverage Index is complete; the DUAL-schema drift residual from prior reviews is now mitigated by an always-on equivalence test. Held below 0.9 because shasum was denied (issues.md SHA == handoff is self_reported) and the live-PG suite is DSN-gated so pytest pass/fail is unconfirmed.

payload.content: issues_review ACCEPT: 4 well-formed slices map P1-P5 via Coverage Index with no orphans; every AC is backed by current source (SQLite default __new__ state.py:292-297, partial-unique idempotency postgres_state.py:147-149 + UniqueViolation reattach:633, MATERIALIZED CTE:19/LIMIT:34/SKIP LOCKED:35/lock-order:17, per-run event_id/previous_event_id:62-63 chain CHECK:70-71 + catch-up:342, migration 20260604_0001 non-vacuous + Makefile:5-7 + config:52-56). Multi-writer double-submit test:262 uses ThreadPoolExecutor(8) DB-enforced (no Python locks); DUAL-schema drift guarded by equivalence test:89.

assumptions_to_verify: issues.md on disk is byte-identical to handoff-declared artifact
Live-PG tests pass when a real Postgres DSN is provided
Inline schema and Alembic migration remain equivalent at runtime
contradictions_checked: DUAL schema (inline postgres_state.py vs migration file) could drift — resolved by always-on equivalence test:89
Whether multi-writer test relies on Python locks vs DB enforcement — confirmed DB-enforced via partial unique + ThreadPoolExecutor
Whether any promise lacks a covering slice — Coverage Index complete, user stories 1-10 all covered
missing_evidence: shasum of issues.md (denied per permission policy)
pytest run output for the live-PG-gated suite
confirmation handoff SHA matches on-disk file
critical_review.strongest_objection: ACs are unchecked spec-form ([ ]) and shasum was denied, so I cannot cryptographically confirm issues.md matches the handoff SHA e605240... nor that the live-PG tests actually pass.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: An uncovered P1-P5 promise, an AC referencing a nonexistent source boundary, the multi-writer test relying on Python locks instead of DB enforcement, or evidence the issues.md on disk diverges from the handoff SHA.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Layer 1 Postgres ledger/job lane with DB-enforced idempotency, SKIP LOCKED multi-claimer claims, and run-partitioned catch-up while keeping SQLite default.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: 10 named plan tests exist verbatim and cover P1-P5/ISS-1..4
always-on tests (sqlite, routing, claim-SQL shape, schema shape, migration files, inline/migration equivalence) statically guard the non-portable concurrency invariants
5 of 10 named tests are live-PG-gated via postgres_state fixture and skip without CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN
grill findings F1-F4 (tdd) resolved and backed by actual test design

criteria: all 10 plan-named tests exist verbatim: PASS
SQL-shape/schema assertions match source substrings: PASS
multi-writer dedup is DB-enforced not Python-lock: PASS
live PG tests executed in this environment: NOT MET (gated)
pytest re-run / RED observed: NOT MET (policy off, GREEN-only)
evidence: accept: 10 plan-named tests present verbatim and map P1-P5/ISS-1..4 with no orphans
accept: SQL-shape asserts (MATERIALIZED CTE, LIMIT-in-CTE, FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, uniform ORDER BY priority/created_at/id) match POSTGRES_CLAIM_AVAILABLE_JOBS_SQL:18 substrings
accept: schema asserts match POSTGRES_SCHEMA_SQL (active partial idempotency idx:147, dispatchable idx:152, events_run_event_unique:68, previous_id CHECK:69) and migration 20260604_0001
accept: multi-writer test :262 uses 8 separate PostgresState connections so dedup is DB-enforced via UniqueViolation reattach reserve:633 not Python lock (resolves grill F2)
accept: routing State.__new__:293 keeps SQLite default (type is State) and routes postgres/postgresql DSNs is_postgres_state_dsn:48 to PostgresState
confidence.rationale: Every plan-named test verified present verbatim and non-vacuous against current source; SQL-shape, schema, migration, routing, and DB-enforced dedup all confirmed by direct read. Confidence held below 0.9 because 5 hardest concurrency tests are live-PG-gated (self_reported here), RED state is unobservable (GREEN-not-RED), and pytest was not re-run per policy.

payload.content: tdd_review ACCEPT. All 10 plan-named tests exist verbatim in tests/test_postgres_ledger_lane.py and map P1-P5/ISS-1..4. SQL-shape (POSTGRES_CLAIM_AVAILABLE_JOBS_SQL:18) and schema (POSTGRES_SCHEMA_SQL:47) assertions match source exactly; migration/inline equivalence guarded by test :89. Multi-writer dedup is DB-enforced (separate connections + partial UNIQUE index + UniqueViolation reattach), non-vacuous. Grill F1-F4 resolved.

assumptions_to_verify: live Postgres run reproduces disjoint-claim and single-active-row results
make migrate / alembic upgrade head succeeds end-to-end in a real PG environment
contradictions_checked: plan test names vs file: all 10 present verbatim, no rename/omission
SQL-shape assertions vs POSTGRES_CLAIM_AVAILABLE_JOBS_SQL: all substrings present, LIMIT precedes ) UPDATE
schema assertions vs POSTGRES_SCHEMA_SQL and migration: partial unique idx, dispatchable idx, CHECK constraint, UNIQUE(run_id,task_id,gate) all present in both
multi-writer test connection model: each thread builds its own PostgresState (apply_schema=False) so per-instance _write_lock cannot serialize across threads — dedup is genuinely DB-enforced
missing_evidence: live pytest run with CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN showing the 5 gated tests pass
observed RED state before implementation
independent shasum reconfirmation of tdd.md / grill-findings-tdd.md (verified by direct read instead)
critical_review.strongest_objection: The five hardest invariants (multi-writer reserve race, SKIP LOCKED disjointness, per-run chain) are live-Postgres-gated and skip without a DSN, and tests pass against already-implemented source (GREEN-not-RED), so RED was never observed at this gate and those invariants are self_reported on a PG-less environment.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A named plan test missing or renamed in the file, an SQL-shape assertion referencing a substring absent from source, or the multi-writer test sharing one connection (which would make dedup Python-lock rather than DB-enforced) — none of which hold.

payload.content:
Independently review the tdd_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Grill F1-F4 TDD resolutions are implemented in test file design

criteria: live PG pytest 14 passed (independent run): PASS
without DSN 6 pass 8 skip (gating as designed): PASS
evidence: accept: 10/10 plan test names present verbatim in tests/test_postgres_ledger_lane.py
accept: live-gated concurrency/idempotency tests skip without DSN and pass with DSN (14 passed independently)
accept: SQL-shape and schema substring guards match POSTGRES_CLAIM_AVAILABLE_JOBS_SQL and POSTGRES_SCHEMA_SQL plus Alembic migration
accept: multi-writer test uses per-thread PostgresState connections; dedup is partial-unique + UniqueViolation not Python lock
accept: grill-findings-tdd F1-F4 resolutions reflected in test design (live+static split, real reserve race, per-run cursor boundary, migration equivalence)

payload.content: tdd_review ACCEPT (Cursor Reviewer). Independently confirmed all 10 plan-named tests verbatim, P1-P5/ISS-1..4 mapping, SQL-shape/schema/migration assertions against source, and DB-enforced multi-writer dedup (separate connections + UniqueViolation reattach). Reproduced receipt: 14 passed live PG, 6 passed/8 skipped without DSN. Residual process gap: GREEN-not-RED only.

assumptions_to_verify: CI or reviewer laptops without CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN rely on static SQL-shape guards for hardest invariants
Alembic upgrade/downgrade remains clean on fresh throwaway DBs outside this session
contradictions_checked: tdd.md 10 test names vs test file: all present verbatim
POSTGRES_CLAIM_AVAILABLE_JOBS_SQL substrings vs test_postgres_claim_sql_uses_fenced_skip_locked_cte: match
POSTGRES_SCHEMA_SQL/migration vs schema tests: partial unique, dispatchable index, events CHECK/UNIQUE present
multi-writer reserve: 8 PostgresState instances with apply_schema=False so per-instance _write_lock cannot cross-thread serialize
receipt pytest-live 14 passed vs independent run: reproduced
missing_evidence: artifact showing tests failed before implementation (RED phase)

critical_review.strongest_objection: GREEN-not-RED: tests were validated against already-green implementation with no recorded failing RED phase at this gate, weakening strict TDD process proof even though behavioral guards are sound.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Any plan-named test missing/renamed, SQL-shape assertion referencing absent source substring, multi-writer test sharing one connection, or live PG pytest failure on the five gated plan tests.

assumptions_to_verify: The live receipt commands in workflow-request-cli-live.json were run against the same current worktree under review
The gate policy permits receipt-backed evidence and does not require the reviewer to rerun live Postgres tests during tdd_review
Production Postgres use is limited to the event/job lane implemented here until remaining SQLite-only tables are explicitly migrated
PgBouncer deployments use default schema or otherwise avoid session search_path assumptions under transaction pooling
contradictions_checked: Claude changed_files omitted pyproject.toml, uv.lock, and migrations/env.py; current tree includes them and they support optional postgres deps plus URL normalization
TDD transcript says 5 live-gated tests; direct rg shows 8 total postgres_state-gated tests, but only 5 are among the 10 plan-named tests
Dedup could have been Python-lock-only; refuted by the test using separate PostgresState connections and the implementation catching UniqueViolation
Claim SQL could over-claim if LIMIT were outside the lock fence; refuted by the MATERIALIZED CTE with ORDER BY, LIMIT, FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED before the UPDATE join
Inline schema and Alembic migration could drift; partially mitigated by the structural-equivalence test and matching inspected snippets
missing_evidence: Fresh local rerun output for CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN live tests in this review turn
Raw stdout/stderr logs for the live Postgres and Alembic upgrade/downgrade receipt commands
Observed RED-before-GREEN history for the named TDD tests
PgBouncer transaction-pool smoke if production will rely on non-default schemas or session search_path
critical_review.strongest_objection: The original tdd_review transcript itself is a static/source review with unknown test evidence refs; the stronger live Postgres and Alembic evidence exists as later receipt records, but I did not see raw command output or rerun it here. Claude also phrases the live-gated count as 5, which is true only for the 10 plan-named tests; the current file has 8 live-gated tests total.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: I would switch to revise if a live Postgres rerun fails, if the Alembic upgrade/downgrade receipt is stale or fails on the current tree, if any plan-named test is missing/renamed, if the claim CTE loses LIMIT-before-UPDATE or SKIP LOCKED, or if PostgresState is expected to replace the full SQLite State interface in this slice.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Layer 1 Postgres ledger/job lane with DB-enforced idempotency, SKIP LOCKED multi-claimer claims, and run-partitioned catch-up while keeping SQLite default.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Layer1 Postgres ledger/job lane implemented with DB-enforced idempotency, SKIP LOCKED multi-claimer claims, run-partitioned catch-up; SQLite remains default
Inline POSTGRES_SCHEMA_SQL and alembic migration are structurally equivalent (test-enforced), prior dual-schema drift residual resolved-as-consistent

criteria: Source diff matches handoff file-ownership exactly
Partial-unique idempotency index present + lockstep across inline and migration
SKIP LOCKED claim SQL fenced (MATERIALIZED CTE, LIMIT-before-UPDATE, uniform order)
Run-partitioned event sequence + previous_event_id chain CHECK present
Always-on tests enforce SQL shape and inline/migration structural equivalence
confidence.rationale: All 4 invariants and file ownership verified by direct full reads of source/migration/tests; SQL shape and inline/migration equivalence covered by always-on tests. Confidence capped below 0.94 because the consequential concurrency behaviors are only covered by live-PG-gated tests that were not executed this gate (pytest un-run, shasum approval-declined), so behavioral correctness is self_reported per the handoff policy.

payload.content: Execution gate ACCEPT. All 4 intent invariants realized at current source: SQLite-default routing (State.__new__:292/is_postgres_state_dsn:48), DB-enforced idempotency via partial-unique index lockstep inline:147/migration:131 + reserve UniqueViolation reattach:633 (8-separate-conn test COUNT==1:294), SKIP LOCKED multi-claimer MATERIALIZED CTE:19 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED:35 uniform ORDER:16 (disjoint 9/9 test:395), run-partitioned catch-up event_stream_sequences PK:55 chain CHECK:69 (test:194). Diff matches handoff exactly; inline/migration structurally equivalent (test:89, no drift). Residual: behavioral tests live-PG-gated + pytest/shasum un-run -> self_reported per policy.

assumptions_to_verify: Live Postgres behavior matches static SQL expectations under real concurrency (SKIP LOCKED, partial-unique violation)
alembic upgrade head applies cleanly against a real PgBouncer/Postgres endpoint
contradictions_checked: Inline POSTGRES_SCHEMA_SQL vs alembic migration tables/indexes/CHECK -- consistent (both include schema_migrations; test:89 enforces equivalence)
Makefile target string vs test assertion test:84 -- present
env.py env-var (DATABASE_URL||POSTGRES_DSN) vs Makefile (DATABASE_URL) -- consistent
double-submit test independence: each thread builds its own PostgresState (separate _write_lock) so UNIQUE index, not python lock, enforces COUNT==1 -- non-vacuous
missing_evidence: pytest run output for the 6 always-on tests
live-PG run output for the 8 gated behavioral tests
shasum confirmation of planning artifacts against handoff (approval declined)
critical_review.strongest_objection: The most consequential guarantees (multi-writer idempotency, SKIP LOCKED disjointness, compare-and-set, run-partitioned catch-up) are validated only by tests gated on CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN, which default CI does not set; combined with pytest being un-run this gate, behavioral correctness is self_reported rather than CI-proven.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the inline schema and migration diverge in a way the equivalence test misses, that reserve/claim SQL fails under real Postgres concurrency, or that __new__ routing breaks the SQLite default path -- any would move from accept toward revise.
